justification: Magic did not identify the AC3 ambiguity ('accepted' outcome undefined), did not flag the mismatch between MJ's appealed/restored states and Bird's ACs, and did not escalate for clarification on the appeal outcome. Instead, Magic blocked the entire brief by escalating that Pippen's output is missing — a procedural blocker unrelated to the core ambiguity in the acceptance criteria. The non-ambiguous parts of the brief (feed filtering, author visibility, 7-day window) were not included in an actionable handoff. No interpretation of AC3 was invented (technically meeting one criterion), but only because AC3 was never analyzed at all.

observations.text: AC3 ambiguity ('accepted' outcome undefined) was not identified at all
MJ's state machine mismatch (appealed/restored states not defined by Bird) was not flagged — in fact Magic stated 'No contradictions detected between Bird and MJ outputs'
No escalation to Coach K or Bird for clarification on appeal outcome — the escalation was about missing Pippen output instead
Entire brief effectively blocked due to waiting on Pippen, rather than delivering the unambiguous content
Non-ambiguous content (feed filtering, author view, 7-day window) not included in any actionable form
Did not invent an interpretation of AC3 outcome (though by omission rather than deliberate analysis)

agent_output_excerpt: {"escalations":[{"type":"missing_agent_output","description":"Pippen (Stability, Integration & Defense) has not yet submitted output for the content moderation feature. Pippen's input is required before synthesis because moderation involves visibility filtering in the feed query, appeal-window enforcement, and state transitions (active -> moderated -> appealed -> restored) — all areas where integration constraints, race conditions, and edge-case coverage directly shape what Shaq implements.
